summary: Descriptive overview of commoninterestcommunities in substantive law: HOAs, covenants, governance structures.
key_points: HOAs is a fundamental concept in property law.
Legal standards for commoninterestcommunities are established through case law and statutory interpretation.
Courts apply specific tests to determine commoninterestcommunities in litigation.
The doctrine of commoninterestcommunities has evolved through Supreme Court precedents.
Understanding commoninterestcommunities requires analysis of both historical context and modern applications.
sources: Supreme Court opinions (Kelo v. New London)
philosophy_guard_statement: This fragment describes existing legal doctrines and their applications without advocating for normative changes.
